notes: L9 'Orientation — 7-line PIL/NumPy preprocessing pipeline (1008.1s, 7 cues)' — Code It From Scratch: PIL/NumPy preprocessing to (1,3,896,896) float32 for Gemma 4 vision. Reuses imgprep.py idiom lib. READ start/mid/end each cue; all legible, in-frame (safe-area guard passed), on-brand (dark palette, blue/orange/green, mono code + serif prose). Math verified: ImageNet mean/std exact, shape transitions (H,W,C)->(C,H,W)->(1,C,H,W) correct, float64 trap correct. 1 -ql pass, no rerender needed. ffprobe within 0.13s of storyboard span.

[end] raw photo -> model-ready tensor: cat.jpg box, 7-step arrow, (1,3,896,896) N/C/H/W tensor, 'no magic library call'

[mid] 7-station assembly line: open, resize, to array, to float, normalize, permute, batch (=7 stations)
[end] 7-station assembly line: open, resize, to array, to float, normalize, permute, batch (=7 stations)

[start] table follows one 2x2 image: Image.open (W,H)=(2,2) PIL, resize (896,896), np.asarray (896,896,3) uint8, /255 float32 0..1, normalize float32 ~-2..2, transpose (3,896,896), [None] (1,3,896,896)
[mid] table follows one 2x2 image: Image.open (W,H)=(2,2) PIL, resize (896,896), np.asarray (896,896,3) uint8, /255 float32 0..1, normalize float32 ~-2..2, transpose (3,896,896), [None] (1,3,896,896)
[end] table follows one 2x2 image: Image.open (W,H)=(2,2) PIL, resize (896,896), np.asarray (896,896,3) uint8, /255 float32 0..1, normalize float32 ~-2..2, transpose (3,896,896), [None] (1,3,896,896)

[mid] three things change: shape (H,W,C)->(C,H,W)->(1,C,H,W), dtype uint8->float32, range 0..255->0..1->normalized
[end] three things change: shape (H,W,C)->(C,H,W)->(1,C,H,W), dtype uint8->float32, range 0..255->0..1->normalized
